root_text.an3.107:1.2: Ummattakamidaṁ, bhikkhave, ariyassa vinaye yadidaṁ naccaṁ.
root_text.an3.107:1.3: Komārakamidaṁ, bhikkhave, ariyassa vinaye yadidaṁ ativelaṁ dantavidaṁsakahasitaṁ.
root_text.an3.107:1.4: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, setughāto gīte, setughāto nacce, alaṁ vo dhammappamoditānaṁ sataṁ sitaṁ sitamattāyā”ti.

translation_text.an3.107:1.1: “Singing is regarded as wailing in the training of the Noble One.
translation_text.an3.107:1.3: Too much laughter, showing the teeth, is regarded as childish.
translation_text.an3.107:1.4: So break off singing and dancing; and when you’re appropriately pleased, it’s enough to simply smile.”
